Internal Working: Called when Angular creates an instance of the class. DI system resolves and injects dependencies. Should NOT contain complex logic.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Initialize services needed by component. Set up form builders.
Example Code: constructor(private http: HttpClient, private router: Router) { }

Purpose: Component initialization after inputs are set
Internal Working: Called once after constructor and after first ngOnChanges. @Input properties are available. Safe to make API calls and set up subscriptions.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Load initial data, subscribe to route changes, initialize forms.
Example Code: ngOnInit(): void { this.searchForm = new FormGroup({ ovAgentNumber: new FormControl('') }); }

Internal Working: Called before ngOnInit and whenever @Input properties change. Receives SimpleChanges object with previous and current values.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Update component when parent passes new data.

Internal Working: Called once when component is about to be destroyed. Must unsubscribe from observables to prevent memory leaks.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Unsubscribe from subscriptions, remove event listeners.

Internal Working: Called once after component's view and child views are initialized. Safe to access @ViewChild elements.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Initialize third-party libraries, access DOM elements.

Internal Working: Called during every change detection run. Use for manual change detection logic. Use sparingly due to performance impact.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Detect changes Angular doesn't catch automatically.

Internal Working: Creates subscription, receives next/error/complete callbacks. Starts observable execution. Returns Subscription object for cleanup.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Handle HTTP responses, process stream data.
Example Code: this.http.get(url).subscribe({ next: (data) => this.results = data, error: (err) => this.handleError(err) });

Internal Working: Disposes subscription, stops receiving values, releases resources. Critical for preventing memory leaks.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Clean up subscriptions in ngOnDestroy.

Internal Working: Creates pipeline of operators that transform, filter, or combine observable emissions. Returns new Observable.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Transform API response, handle errors, log data.

Internal Working: Applies function to each emission, returns new Observable with transformed values. Does not modify original.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Transform API response to component model.

Internal Working: Only emits values that pass provided predicate function. Skips values that don't match.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Filter navigation events, process only specific events.

Purpose: Switch to new observable, cancel previous
Internal Working: Maps to inner Observable, subscribes, cancels previous inner subscription when new outer value arrives. Prevents race conditions.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Chain dependent HTTP calls, cancel outdated requests.

Internal Working: Maps to inner Observable, subscribes, allows concurrent subscriptions. Does not cancel previous.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Process multiple parallel requests.

Internal Working: Catches errors from source Observable, returns new Observable or throws. Prevents stream termination.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Handle API errors, provide fallback values.

Internal Working: Performs side effects for each emission without modifying values. Used for logging, state updates.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Log API responses, update loading state.

Purpose: Take first N values then complete
Internal Working: Emits only first N values, then completes. Useful for auto-completing subscriptions.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Get single value from stream, auto-unsubscribe.

Internal Working: Creates Observable that emits provided values synchronously, then completes. Useful for mock data.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Return fallback value on error, mock data.

Internal Working: Creates Observable that immediately emits error notification. Used to propagate errors.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Throw validation errors from service.
Example Code: if (!userId) { return throwError(() => new Error('User ID required')); }

Internal Working: Requires initial value, emits current value to new subscribers, stores latest value.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Share state across components, loading indicators.
Example Code: private loading$ = new BehaviorSubject<boolean>(false); show() { this.loading$.next(true); }

Internal Working: Creates GET request, returns Observable. Supports headers, params, response type options.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Fetch data from REST API.

Internal Working: Creates POST request with body, returns Observable. Used to create resources.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Create new record via API.

Internal Working: Creates PUT request with body, returns Observable. Used to update resources.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Update existing record via API.

Internal Working: Creates DELETE request, returns Observable. Used to remove resources.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Delete record via API.

Internal Working: Navigates to URL/route. Accepts array of path segments, extras (queryParams, state).
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Navigate after form submission, redirect on error.

Internal Working: Retrieves parameter value from route URL. Returns string or null.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Get record ID from URL for edit page.

Internal Working: Called before route activation. Returns boolean, Observable, or UrlTree. Blocks or allows navigation.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Check authentication and authorization.

Internal Working: Intercepts outgoing requests and incoming responses. Can modify headers, handle errors globally.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Add auth tokens, log requests, handle errors.

Internal Working: Creates writable signal with initial value. Consumers auto-update when value changes.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Manage component state reactively.

Internal Working: Updates signal to new value, notifies all consumers. Triggers change detection.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Update state after API response.

Internal Working: Updates signal using function that receives current value. Useful for modifications.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Append to existing array.

Internal Working: Creates read-only signal derived from other signals. Auto-updates when dependencies change.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Calculate derived values.

Internal Working: Executes side effects when dependent signals change. Runs in injection context.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Log changes, sync with external systems.

Internal Working: Retrieves dependency from injector. Modern alternative to constructor injection. Must be in injection context.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Get services in component or function.
Example Code: private readonly router = inject(Router); private readonly http = inject(HttpClient);

Internal Working: Container for FormControls. Tracks value, validity, dirty state of group. Enables validation.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Create forms with multiple fields.
Example Code: this.form = new FormGroup({ name: new FormControl(''), email: new FormControl('') });

Internal Working: Tracks value and validation state of single input. Supports validators, disabled state.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Bind to input fields.

Internal Working: Updates specified controls without requiring all controls. Does not reset untouched controls.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Update form with partial data.

Internal Working: Sets values for all controls. Requires all controls to be provided. Validates structure.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Reset form with complete data.

Internal Working: Resets all controls to initial values, clears dirty/touched states.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Clear form after submission.

Internal Working: Returns true if all controls pass validation. Used before submission.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Validate before API call.

Internal Working: Checks if user is authenticated, validates tokens, returns LoginResponse observable.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Initialize auth on app startup.

Internal Working: Redirects to identity provider for authentication. Starts OAuth/OIDC flow.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Trigger login on button click.

Internal Working: Clears tokens, ends session, redirects to post-logout URL. Returns observable.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Handle logout action.

Internal Working: Retrieves stored access token. Returns Observable<string>. Used in interceptors.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Add token to API requests.

Internal Working: Combines base URL from environment with endpoint enum. Centralizes URL construction.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Construct API URLs consistently.

Internal Working: Creates modal dialog with OK/Cancel buttons. Returns Promise<boolean> with user choice.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Confirm before delete operation.
Example Code: const confirmed = await this.confirmDialogService.confirm('Delete this record?');

Internal Working: Sets loading state to true. Components subscribe to show spinner.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Show spinner during API calls.

Internal Working: Sets loading state to false. Hides spinner component.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Hide spinner after API completes.

Internal Working: Checks if user's screen codes include required screen code. Returns boolean.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Guard route based on permissions.

Internal Working: Calls API to get user's screen codes. Stores in signal for reactive access.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Load permissions after login.

Internal Working: Returns array of screens user can access. Used for sidebar navigation.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Build navigation menu.

Internal Working: Angular evaluates expression and converts to string. Updates automatically on change detection. One-way binding from component to view.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Display dynamic text, show API results, display error/success messages.

Internal Working: Calls signal getter function to get current value. Triggers re-render when signal changes. Used with Angular Signals.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Display reactive state values like search results, loading state, errors.

Internal Working: Accesses property of object. Use safe navigation (?.) for nullable objects. Can chain multiple levels.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Display record fields in table rows, show user data.

Internal Working: Uses JavaScript OR operator to provide default when expression is null/undefined/empty.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Show 'N/A' when data is missing, provide placeholder text.

Internal Working: Evaluates condition, displays first value if true, second if false. Inline conditional logic.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Show different text based on state, toggle icons.

Internal Working: Interpolates class name from constants object. Ensures consistent styling across app.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Apply Bolt Design System classes consistently.

Internal Working: Calls component method and displays return value. Re-evaluates on each change detection cycle.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Get dynamic CSS classes, format display values.

Purpose: Bind component property to element property
Internal Working: One-way binding from component to DOM property. Angular updates DOM when value changes. Uses square brackets.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Set element properties dynamically like disabled, src, href.

Internal Working: Connects FormGroup instance to form element. Enables reactive forms functionality.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Connect form object to HTML form element.

Internal Working: Sets disabled attribute based on expression result. Prevents user interaction when true.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Disable buttons during loading, disable invalid form submission.

Internal Working: Adds/removes CSS classes based on object keys (class names) with boolean values.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Apply styling based on state, alternate row colors.

Internal Working: Adds class when condition is true, removes when false. Simpler than ngClass for single class.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Highlight active menu item, show selected state.

Internal Working: Binds expression result to input element's value property. Used with checkboxes, hidden inputs.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Set checkbox values, hidden field values for forms.

Internal Working: Listens for DOM event, calls component method when triggered. One-way from view to component.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Handle clicks, form submissions, keyboard events.

Internal Working: Calls component method when element is clicked. Can pass $event or custom parameters.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Button actions, navigation, toggle states.

Internal Working: Fires when form is submitted. Works with reactive forms. Prevents default form behavior.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Process form data, call API with form values.

Internal Working: Fires on each key press. Receives KeyboardEvent. Used for input validation/filtering.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Validate alphanumeric input, prevent special characters.

Internal Working: Connects input element to named FormControl in FormGroup. Enables two-way reactive binding.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Link form inputs to reactive form controls.

Internal Working: Adds/removes element from DOM based on condition. Element not created when false (better performance).
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Show/hide messages, conditional UI sections.

Internal Working: Creates element for each item in array. Provides index, first, last, even, odd variables.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Render table rows, list items, menu options.
Example Code: *ngFor=let result of searchResults(); let i = index

Internal Working: New control flow syntax. Requires track expression for performance. Cleaner than *ngFor.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Iterate over collections with better performance.

Internal Working: New control flow syntax. Cleaner than *ngIf. Supports @else blocks.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Conditional rendering with modern syntax.

Internal Working: Shows sidebar only when user is authenticated and on protected route. Uses signal value.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Toggle layout sections based on auth state.

Internal Working: Shows success message after successful update operation.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Feedback to user after form submission.

Internal Working: Shows error message for validation failures or API errors.
Real-Time Usage Scenario: Display validation or server errors.
